root_text.an3.3:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bālo duccintitacintī ca hoti dubbhāsitabhāsī ca dukkaṭakammakārī ca.
root_text.an3.3:1.4: No cedaṁ, bhikkhave, bālo duccintitacintī ca abhavissa dubbhāsitabhāsī ca dukkaṭakammakārī ca, kena naṁ paṇḍitā jāneyyuṁ:
root_text.an3.3:1.6: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, bālo duccintitacintī ca hoti dubbhāsitabhāsī ca dukkaṭakammakārī ca tasmā naṁ paṇḍitā jānanti:
root_text.an3.3:1.8: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi bālassa bālalakkhaṇāni bālanimittāni bālāpadānāni.
root_text.an3.3:2.1: Tīṇimāni, bhikkhave, paṇḍitassa paṇḍitalakkhaṇāni paṇḍitanimittāni paṇḍitāpadānāni.
root_text.an3.3:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, paṇḍito sucintitacintī ca hoti subhāsitabhāsī ca sukatakammakārī ca.
root_text.an3.3:2.4: No cedaṁ, bhikkhave, paṇḍito sucintitacintī ca abhavissa subhāsitabhāsī ca sukatakammakārī ca, kena naṁ paṇḍitā jāneyyuṁ:
root_text.an3.3:2.6: Yasmā ca kho, bhikkhave, paṇḍito sucintitacintī ca hoti subhāsitabhāsī ca sukatakammakārī ca tasmā naṁ paṇḍitā jānanti:
root_text.an3.3:2.8: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, tīṇi paṇḍitassa paṇḍitalakkhaṇāni paṇḍitanimittāni paṇḍitāpadānāni.

translation_text.an3.3:1.1: “There are these three characteristics, signs, and manifestations of a fool.
translation_text.an3.3:1.3: A fool thinks poorly, speaks poorly, and acts poorly.
translation_text.an3.3:1.4: If a fool didn’t think poorly, speak poorly, and act poorly, then how would the astute know of them:
translation_text.an3.3:1.5: ‘This worthy one is a fool, an untrue person’?
translation_text.an3.3:1.6: But since a fool does think poorly, speak poorly, and act poorly, then the astute do know of them:
translation_text.an3.3:1.7: ‘This worthy one is a fool, an untrue person’.
translation_text.an3.3:1.8: These are the three characteristics, signs, and manifestations of a fool.
translation_text.an3.3:2.1: There are these three characteristics, signs, and manifestations of an astute person.
translation_text.an3.3:2.3: An astute person thinks well, speaks well, and acts well.
translation_text.an3.3:2.4: If an astute person didn’t think well, speak well, and act well, then how would the astute know of them:
translation_text.an3.3:2.5: ‘This worthy one is astute, a true person’?
translation_text.an3.3:2.6: But since an astute person does think well, speak well, and act well, then the astute do know of them:
translation_text.an3.3:2.7: ‘This worthy one is astute, a true person’.
translation_text.an3.3:2.8: These are the three characteristics, signs, and manifestations of an astute person.

variant_text.an3.3:1.4: cedaṁ → no cetaṁ (sya-all, km, mr) | kena naṁ paṇḍitā jāneyyuṁ → tena naṁ paṇḍitā na jāneyyuṁ (mr); na naṁ paṇḍitā jāneyyuṁ (?)
